clause: The employee shall be entitled to a notice period of thirty days before termination.
Either party may terminate this agreement with sixty days written notice.
Termination for cause shall be effective immediately upon written notice.
In the event of termination, all outstanding payments shall be settled within seven days.
The company reserves the right to terminate employment without notice during the probation period.
The probation period shall be six months from the date of joining.
During probation, the employee is not entitled to any paid leave benefits.
The employee will be confirmed on successful completion of the probation period.
Performance during probation will be evaluated by the line manager monthly.
The monthly salary shall be paid on or before the fifth of each month.
Payment of invoices shall be made within thirty days of receipt.
The vendor shall raise invoices on the first of each month for services rendered.
Late payments shall attract interest at the rate of eighteen percent per annum.
All payments shall be made in Indian Rupees via NEFT or RTGS transfer.
The service fee shall be paid in advance on a quarterly basis.
GST as applicable shall be added to all invoiced amounts.
All intellectual property created during employment belongs exclusively to the company.
The employee assigns all rights in any invention made during employment to the employer.
Any software, code, or design created during the term of this contract is the property of the client.
The vendor retains no rights to any deliverable produced under this agreement.
Pre-existing intellectual property of the vendor is not transferred under this agreement.
The employee shall maintain strict confidentiality of all proprietary information.
The receiving party shall not disclose any confidential information to third parties.
Confidential information includes trade secrets, client lists, and pricing strategies.
The obligation of confidentiality shall survive termination of this agreement by two years.
Confidential information shall not be used for any purpose other than the scope of this agreement.
The employee shall not engage in any competing business during employment.
For a period of one year after termination, the employee shall not solicit clients of the company.
The non-compete restriction applies within a radius of fifty kilometers of the employer's registered office.
The employee agrees not to join any direct competitor within twelve months of separation.
The liability of either party under this agreement shall not exceed the total contract value.
Neither party shall be liable for indirect, consequential, or punitive damages.
The maximum liability of the service provider shall be limited to three months of fees paid.
The vendor shall indemnify the client against all third-party claims arising from negligence.
Force majeure events shall excuse delay in performance but not payment obligations.
Any dispute arising from this agreement shall be resolved through arbitration.
The courts of Chennai shall have exclusive jurisdiction over all matters under this agreement.
Disputes shall first be attempted to be resolved through mediation within thirty days.
The arbitration shall be conducted under the Arbitration and Conciliation Act 1996.
The maintenance team shall respond to critical issues within four hours of reporting.
The service provider shall maintain the system with ninety-nine point five percent uptime.
Scheduled maintenance shall be carried out only during off-peak hours on weekends.
The vendor warrants that all deliverables shall be free from material defects for twelve months.
The service provider warrants that the software will perform as specified in the scope of work.
All hardware supplied under this agreement carries a standard manufacturer warranty.
The warranty period for civil works shall be five years from the date of completion.
This agreement shall be governed by the laws of India.
This agreement constitutes the entire understanding between the parties.
Any amendment to this agreement must be made in writing and signed by both parties.
Notices shall be sent to the registered addresses of the parties via registered post.
This agreement is binding upon the heirs, successors, and assigns of the parties.
The employee shall comply with all applicable laws and company policies.
Working hours shall be from nine in the morning to six in the evening, Monday through Friday.
The vendor shall provide monthly status reports on project progress.
The annual increment shall be subject to performance review and company discretion.
Travel expenses shall be reimbursed as per the company travel policy.
The employee shall be eligible for an annual bonus based on performance.
Gratuity shall be payable as per the Payment of Gratuity Act 1972.
Provident fund contributions shall be deducted from the employee salary as per EPF Act.
The lease shall be for a period of eleven months and may be renewed by mutual consent.
Either party may terminate the lease by giving two months written notice.
The tenant shall vacate the premises within thirty days of receipt of termination notice.
The security deposit shall be refunded within thirty days of vacation of premises.
The monthly rent shall be increased by five percent on each renewal.
Maintenance charges shall be borne equally by landlord and tenant.
The tenant shall not sublet the premises without written consent of the landlord.
The landlord shall ensure all structural repairs are completed within a reasonable time.
All source code shall be delivered with complete documentation and unit test coverage.
Acceptance testing shall be completed within fifteen business days of delivery.
The service provider shall not disclose client data to any third party.
Data breach notification shall be made within seventy-two hours of discovery.
The client data shall be processed only for the purposes specified in this agreement.
The SLA shall include response time, resolution time, and uptime guarantees.
Service credits shall be issued for any breach of the uptime guarantee.
The service provider shall maintain adequate insurance coverage during the term.
Background verification shall be completed before the employee joins.
The employee must complete all mandatory training within the first month.
The vendor must comply with all applicable data protection regulations.
All software licenses required for this project shall be procured by the client.
The employee shall not take up any secondary employment without prior approval.
Any information regarding pricing strategies is strictly confidential.
The vendor warrants that all work is original and does not infringe third-party rights.
Damages for breach of this agreement shall be assessed at the time of the breach.
The employer shall provide a safe working environment as per applicable labor laws.
The employee shall follow the code of conduct as outlined in the employee handbook.
The company shall not discriminate based on gender, caste, religion, or disability.
Any harassment complaint shall be addressed as per the POSH Act 2013 policy.
Disputes shall be settled by a sole arbitrator appointed by mutual agreement.
The seat of arbitration shall be Bengaluru, Karnataka, India.
Both parties shall bear their own legal costs unless otherwise ordered by the arbitrator.
Performance appraisals shall be conducted annually in the month of April.
Key deliverables and timelines are as set forth in Schedule A of this agreement.
The escrow release conditions are defined in the payment schedule annexed hereto.
The renewal of this agreement shall require written consent from both parties thirty days in advance.
The company shall provide group medical insurance for employees and their dependents.
The vendor shall not assign this agreement to any third party without prior written consent.
